title: Deaths per year, by age group
caption:
Deaths per year, by age group, World
Annual number of deaths worldwide, broken down by age group

This stacked area chart tracks the annual number of deaths worldwide from 1950 to 2023, categorized into ten distinct age groups ranging from under 9 years old to 100 years and older. The vertical axis measures deaths in millions, scaling from 0 to 70 million, while the horizontal axis spans the mid-20th century to the present. The data shows a general upward trend in total global deaths over the seventy-three-year period, rising from just under 50 million in 1950 to over 60 million in 2023, with a distinct spike occurring around 2020 where the total briefly approaches 70 million. The largest component throughout the timeline is the "Ages <9" group, represented by the bottom blue layer; this segment begins above 20 million in 1950 and steadily declines to approximately 6 million by 2023. Conversely, the layers representing older populations have expanded significantly. The "Ages 70-79" group, shown in dark blue, and the "Ages 80-89" group, in teal, both show substantial growth in thickness, particularly after 1990. By 2023, the "Ages 70-79" band is one of the widest segments, indicating a high volume of deaths in this cohort. The "Ages 60-69" group in pink also maintains a consistent presence, while the middle bands for ages 30 to 59 show a gradual narrowing over time. The uppermost layers for ages 90 and above remain thin but visible, showing a slight increase in recent decades. The chart illustrates a structural shift where the absolute number of deaths among young children has fallen, while the number of deaths among the elderly has risen, contributing to the overall increase in global mortality figures.
